title: fraxinus longicuspis
intro: Fraxinus longicuspis, also known as the Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus longicuspis include pain relief, fever reduction, and treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. Fraxinus longicuspis is typically prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for use in traditional herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
